blockingIssues.description: `checkoutRef()` now runs `git checkout -- <ref>`, which switches Git into pathspec mode and breaks ordinary branch/tag/SHA checkout instead of safely checking out refs.

`redactCredentials()` still stops at the first `@` inside a credential segment, so auth-failure stderr can leak password suffixes such as `ssw0rd` from URLs like `https://admin:p@ssw0rd@git.example.com/repo.git`.

appliedUpdates.description: Added an architecture note that shell-safety fixes for git command construction must be verified with isolated real git-behavior checks, not only mocked SSH command-string assertions.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to mark `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` whenever implementation starts before tests or required manual SSH verification is skipped, and require the deviation to be recorded in the handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All four rerun reviews (`fix-git-ops-security`, `fix-git-ops-errors`, `fix-skill-ops-portability`, and `fix-skill-ops-edge-cases`) observed transcript/handoff mismatches where workers reported `followedProcedure: true` despite code-before-tests and/or missing manual SSH verification.

rejectedObservations.observation: Three additional rerun reviews repeated the same backend-worker procedure/handoff mismatch already captured in the synthesized guidance recommendation.
